msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME LP 491; Grid 480 407 (Lincs: Hand A); 1.144 (Leics: Hand B, ff. 198-219v); LP 492; Grid 518 406 (Lincs: Hand C, ff. 232v-266r); 1.144 (Lincs: Hand D, f. 266v); 1.144 (Lincs: Hand E, ff. 267-276v, 279-285v).
LALME and eLALME LP 498; Grid 415 370 (Derbys: Hand A, ff. 15-64v); LP 167; Grid 377 414 (Lancs: Hand B, ff. 117-136); LP 259; Grid 362 443 (Lancs: also Hand B, ff. 139-147v).
London? See , p. 160. Some of the marked forms suggest Norfolk (see ).
LALME and eLALME LP 1.152 ('Language of NE Lincs, mixed with a Leics component').
LALME and eLALME 1.152 ('Language probably somewhat mixed, but clearly NE Midland and possibly of Rutland': ff. 15-16).
LAEME Index # 175; Grid 565 313 (NW Norfolk: Hand B, f. 145vb).
LALME and eLALME LP 5480; Grid 432 133 (Hants: Hand A, ff. 11v-22v); LP 8491; Grid 591 274 (Suffolk: Hand B, ff. 42-48
LAEME Index # 176 ('text language not placed — too little to go on').

LALME and eLALME 1.145 (Midland: Hand A, pp. 2-31); 1.145 (NME: Hand B, p. 33); 1.145 (Notts: Hand C, pp. 39-50); LP 908 (Lincs: Hand D, pp. 57-71); 1.145 (Cambs: Hand E, pp. 81-92); 1.145 (NME: Hand F, pp. 93-107); LP 398; Grid 455 439 (WRY: Hand G, p. 107-129); 1.145 (Midland: Hand H, pp. 131-139); 1.145 (Southerly language: Hand J, pp. 1-52, 55-64); 1.145 (Midland: Hand K, pp. 53-54); LP 357 (Staffs: Hand L, ff.[sic?] 67-80, 83-end).
LALME and eLALME LP 7680; Grid 391 260 (Worcs: ff. 4-12); LP 277; Grid 496 353 (Lincs: ff. 36-41v).
LAEME Index # 151; Grid 385 433 (Lancs, Hand D, ff. 107-108).
LAEME Index # 18 ('text language not placed — too little to go on').
LALME and eLALME 1.145 ('Language probably of NE Leics, but possibly from SE Notts or SW Lincs').

LALME and eLALME LP 5651; Grid 496 137 (Surrey: Hand A, ff. 134-141; Hand B, ff. 141v-143; Hand C, ff. 143v-144; Hand D, ff. 145-151); LP 5840; Grid 537 123 (Sussex: Hand F, ff. 155-167); LP 5660; Grid 488 127 (Sussex: Hand G, ff. 168-172).
LALME and eLALME 1.146 (Cheshire: Hand A, ff. 2-6, 6v, 14v, 16; Hand B, f. 6r-v); LP 726; Grid 383 349 (Staffs: Hand D, ff. 86v-97v).
LALME and eLALME 1.146 (Cambs: part B, f. 182v seq.); LP 640 (Norfolk: Part E, ff. 355-414).
LALME and eLALME LP 6930; Grid 418 235 (Gloucs); LAEME Grid 418 235 (NE Gloucs).
LALME and eLALME LP 7700 (Worcs: Hand A, beginning to f. 139); 1.146 ('Language of Suffolk or SE Norfolk: Hand B, ff. 139-140); 1.146 (Worcs Hand C, ff. 141-208).

LAEME Index # 178 ('text language not placed — too little to go on': Entry 1, Hand A, f. 6); Index # 179 ('text language not placed — too little to go on': Entry 2, Hand B, f. 6v); Index # 180 ('text language not placed — too little to go on': Entry 3, Hand B, f. 15); Index # 181 ('text language not placed — too little to go on': Entry 4, Hand C, f. 111).
LAEME Index # 14 (' text language not placed — too little to go on').

LALME and eLALME LP 7790 (Gloucs); LAEME Index # 2002; Grid 375 232 (Redmarley D'Abitot, NW Gloucs: Entry 1, ff. 119-120, 120v-12v, 122v-125v, 125v-126v, 126v-127, 127r-v, 127v-130, 130-132, 134v-136v, 136v-138, 138-140, 163v-164, 168r-v, 197v-198, 198-200, 206); Index # 214 ('text language not placed. The language is mixed, though the elements that do not match the SW Midland usage of the Digby scribe do not form a sufficiently coherent set to be localisable '); Entry 2, ff. 132-134v); Index # 218 ('text language not placed. The language is mixed, showing elements of NE Midland forms along with the SW Midland usage of the Digby scribe': Entry 3, ff. 140v-143); Index # 220 ('text language not placed. The language is mixed, showing elements of NE Midland forms along with the SW Midland usage of the Digby scribe. The NE Midland element is quite closely localisable to an area comprising central Notts and SW Lincs, perhaps the area of the poem's original composition': Entry 4, ff. 165-168); Index # 222 ('text language not placed. The language is mixed. The relicts that do not match the SW Midland usage of the Digby scribe fall into two groups: a northerly or NE Midland strand, and a set of forms that are not northerly but are otherwise widespread in early Middle English': Entry 5, ff. 195v-197v).
LALME and eLALME 1.147 (Lincs, Leics: Hand B, ff. 54-93).
LALME and eLALME LP 7710; Grid 382 259 (Worcs: Hand A, ff. 1-9); LP 8110; Grid 450 263 (Warwicks: Hand B, ff. 9 to end).
LALME and eLALME LP 412 (NRY: Hand A, f. 1-162v); 1.147 (NRY: Hand B, f. 162v to end).
LALME and eLALME 1.147 (Cambs: Hand B, f. 9 seq.).
LALME and eLALME LP 5831; Grid 542 136 (Sussex: ff. 30v-52).
LALME and eLALME LP 5720; Grid 517 130 (Sussex: Hand A, ff. 1-24).
LALME and eLALME 1.147 (Northants: Hand E, 'See Douce 60'); LP 515; Grid 417 318 (Staffs: Part C, Hand A, ff. 52-58).
LALME and eLALME LP 8050; Grid 432 243 (Warwicks: Hand A, ff. 1-96v); LP 6890; Grid 438 233 (Oxon: Hand B, ff. 97-129v).
LALME and eLALME 1.147 ('S Lincs with a slight Essex overlay': ff. 1-23v); LP 6330; Grid 549 191 (Essex: ff. 24v-113v).
LALME and eLALME LP 189; Grid 383 336 (Staffs: Hand A, ff. 1-34v); 1.148 (Staffs: Hand B, f. 35r and corrector).
LALME and eLALME 1.148 (Norfolk: Hand A, ff. 11-50).

custodialHist.custEvent: Fols 3–4 formerly in the binding of printed book 8o G 40 Med (Thomas Bricot, Paris 1523). Removed and joined to the present shelfmark in 1967.

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME LP 4245; Grid 383 284 (Staffs: ff. 1-63); 1.149 (Salop: Hand B, f. 12v).
LALME and eLALME LP 32; Grid 400 451 (WRY: Hand A, beginning to f. 208); LP 592; Grid 403 461 (WRY: Hand B, f. 208-212)
LALME and eLALME LP 104 (Cheshire: ff. 92v-95, 101v, 103v, 104, 106-111v); 1.149 (Cheshire: ff. 123, 124v-127).

LALME and eLALME 1.149 (Gloucs, East Anglia: part III, ff. 228v to end ['The language is mixed, and suggests a western (probably Gloucs) original, with an East Anglian overlay']); LAEME Index # 1600; Grid 429 221 (W Oxon: Entry 1, Hand A, ff. 1-10v, 11-22, 23-198, 198r-v, 199-200); Index # 282; Grid 556 280 (Isle of Ely, Cambs: Entry 2, Hand B, ff. 200v-203v); Index # 285; Grid 554 300 (W Norfolk: Entry 3, Hand C, ff. 204-219v, 219v-228).

LALME and eLALME LP 260; Grid 406 330 (Staffs: Hand B, ff. 2v-5v); LP 177; Grid 407 338 (Staffs: Hand C, ff. 5v-34v); LP 303; Grid 418 387 (Derbys: Hand D, ff. 35-36v, 126-127v); LP 262 (WRY: Hand E, ff. 36v-125v); 1.150 (Derbys: Hand G, ff, 127v-end).
LALME and eLALME LP 6050; Grid 562 174 (Kent); LAEME Index #141 ('text language not placed — probably mixed': Entry 1, f. 65); Index # 142; Grid 583 153 (Central Kent: Entry 2, ff. 92 seq.).
LALME and eLALME LP 4567; Grid 578 324 (Norfolk: ff. 62-92); 1.150 (Norfolk: f. 92r-v).
LALME and eLALME LP 7310; Grid 359 221 (Herfs: Hand C, ff. 31-37 41v-42v, 45-63, 69-72v); LP 6980; Grid 393 198 (Gloucs: Hand D, ff. 30v, 38-41, 43-44v, 63v-68, 73-74v).
notes a mixture of London forms with marked forms of the 'North-East Midlands' (pp. 153-4).
LALME and eLALME 1.150 (Lincs: Hand A, ff. 1-57v).
LALME and eLALME LP 44; Grid 437 319 (Leics: Hand A, ff. 98v-109); 1.150 ('Language probably of Staffs': Hand B, ff. 144r-v, 156-163v).
A Western layer likely derived from the exemplar, along with a Northern layer that is arguably scribal; see , pp. 154-5.
LALME and eLALME 1.150 ('Language probably of NW Lincs or NE Notts': hand A, ff. 1-44v).

history.provenance: Robinson Sale Cat. No. 50, Item l0; Robinson Sale Cat. 62, Item 125

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME 1.150 ('Language looks to be of NW Gloucs': Hand A, ff. 1-99, 110-115v); 1.150 (Midland: Hand B, ff. 99v-109v).
LALME and eLALME 1.148 (Notts: Hand A, ff. 1-108, 140-172).
LALME and eLALME 1.150 (Staffs: Hand A, ff. 13-20); LP 21 (Lancs: Hand B, ff. 77-81); 1.150 (Notts: Hand B, ff. 81-84).
LAEME Index # 16; Grid 344 452 (Cockersand Abbey, NW Lancs).
LALME and eLALME 1.151 (Northants: Hand B, ff. 4-34v); LP 171; Grid 369 473 (WRY: Hand C, ff. 35-111v); 1.151 (Lincs: Hand D, ff. 111v-127).
LALME and eLALME 1.151 ('Language of Soke': f. 9r-v); 1.151 (Norfolk: ff. 44-51).
LAEME Index # 228 ('text language not placed — idiosyncratic and too little to go on').
Thames Estuary, with forms found in Kent and Essex (see and , pp. 162-3.)
LALME and eLALME LP 4684; Grid 425 252 (Warwicks: ff. 93-140v).
LALME and eLALME LP 7090; Grid 384 219 (Gloucs); LAEME Index # 229 (Llanthony Priory, Gloucs).
LALME and eLALME 1.152 (Lincs: ff. 150-180); LP 197; Grid 458 485 (NRY: ff. 239-250); LP 7; Grid 458 485 (NRY: ff. 252-260); 1.152 (Lincs: ff. 260 seq.).
argue for a London scribe copying a Western exemplar.
LALME and eLALME 1.153 (Lincs: Hand A, ff. 1v-3; Hand B, f. 155v).
LALME and eLALME LP 7440; Grid 372 244 (Herefords); LAEME Index # 1100; Grid 372 244 (E Herefords: Part II, ff. 144-195, 198-200v).
LALME and eLALME 1.153 ('Norfolk associations. Norfolk'). But cf. , where it is suggested the manuscript's marked spellings point to the Southwest.
LALME and eLALME 1.153 (Staffs: Hand A, ff. 3-8v, 30v, 53-78v, 84r-v, 11v-114); 1.153-4 ('Language of Lichfield, Staffs: Hand B, ff. 8v-30v, 31v-53, 79-83v, 111).

East Anglia, possibly Cambridgeshire or Ely (see and , p. 149).

'South Lincolnshire, or possibly North Norfolk' (, p. 152).

LAEME Index # 4; Grid 557 222 (Entry 1, W Essex: Hand A); Index # 1200; Grid 552 238 (Entry 2, NW Essex: Hand A ['The language of Hand B, which divides into two slightly differing kinds, belongs further north than that of hand A, probably in west Suffolk near the Cambridgeshire border. The language of hand C probably belongs in W Berks']); Index # 1300; Grid 571 267 (Entry 3, W Suffolk: Hand B); Index # 63; Grid 427 185 (Entry 4, W Berks: Hand C).

Likely London, with residual Western forms from the exemplar (Gloucestershire/Herefordshire: , pp. 146-7).

custodialHist.custEvent: Destroyed by fire; transcribed by William Thomas, 1721.

LAEME Index # 118; Grid 370 349 (S Cheshire: Entry 1, Ancrene Riwle); Index # 119 ('text language not placed — language mixed': Entry 2, ff. 40ra line 1 word 7–40vb line 6; 44vb line 22–46rb line 26; 52va line 17–54ra line 25; 56va line 7–61rb line 22; 67rb line 17–68ra line 2; 69ra line 2–70ra line 1); Index # 120 ('text language not placed — language mixed': Entry 2, ff. 105v–112v, Sawles Warde); Index # 121 ('text language not placed — language mixed': Entry 3, ff. 112v–127, Hali Meiðhad); Index # 122; Grid 391 389 (NE Cheshire: Entry 4, ff. 127–133, Þe Wohunge of Ure Lauerd); Index # 123 ('text language not placed — language mixed': Entry 5, ff. 133v–147v, St Katherine).

LALME and eLALME LP 18; Grid 388 447 (WRY: ff. 2-91v); LP 100; Grid 430 398 (WRY: ff. 92-93v); LAEME Index # 295; Grid 388 447 (Yorkshire, West Riding: Hand A: ff. 2r–91v; 93va line 9–95vb line 19; 99ra–112vb; 119rb–139va line 36 Cursor Mundi and fols. 139va line 37–163ra: Exposition of the Creed, Lord's Prayer and Exposition, Prayer for the Hours of the Passion, Prayer to the Trinity, The Book of Penance).
LALME and eLALME LP 7120; Grid 399 233 (Gloucs); LAEME Index # 271 (N Gloucs: ff. 6r–8v Floriz and Blauncheflur).

LAEME Index # 234; Grid 390 232 (N Gloucs: Entry 1, Hand A, f. 1v. 'The language of all the English texts probably belongs in the SW Worcs or [as this one] just over the border into N Gloucs. But for three of the hands there is too little evidence to be sure.'); Index # 235 ('text language not placed': Entry 2, Hand B, f. 2); Index # 236 ('text language not placed': Entry 3, Hand C, f. 2r-v); Index # 237 ('text language not placed': Entry 4, Hand D, f. 2v); Index # 7; Grid 378 246 (SW Worcs: Entry 5, Hand F, ff. 7–12v); Index # 6; Grid 385 239 (SW Worcs: Entry 6, Hand G, ff. 64-70v).
LALME and eLALME LP 6150; Grid 589 227 (Essex); see also Mosser 1997.

LALME and eLALME LP 7020; Grid 589 227 (Gloucs: ff. 1-87v); 1.109 (Cheshire or S Lancs: ff. 46r-v, 6r-v); LP 7110; Grid 408 229 (Gloucs: ff. 88-94, 95-99. 162-179v); 'language possibly of N. Lancs': f. 99v); Cheshire: ff. 163v-164, 170r-v); LP 495; Grid 393 416 (Lancs: ff. 180v-181).
suggests 'the Southwest (Wiltshire, Somerset, and Hampshire)', but cf. , where 'North Norfolk or Lincolnshire' is posited (p. 149).
LALME and eLALME LP 8301; Grid 569 250 (Suffolk); see also Mosser 2005
LALME and eLALME LP 7500; Grid 363 258 (Herefords: Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle, Hand A); LP 7080; Grid 384 219 (Gloucs: Robert of Gloucester's Chronicle, Hand B).

LALME and eLALME 1.110 ('Language appears to be of Staffs, but mixed': ff. 21v-23v).
LALME and eLALME 1.110 ('…language is from Lichfield or nearby. Staffs').
LALME and eLALME 1.110 ('Language of S Rutland, probably SW rather than SE': ff. 1-15); 1.110 ('Possibly Rutland language': ff. 15-22v); LP 626 (Norfolk: ff. 63 to end).

LALME and eLALME 1.111 ('Hands A and B are similar linguistically, of the NE Midlands and possibly of Rutland').
LALME and eLALME 1.111 ('Language mainly standard, but with a few possibly northern (or Lincs) features.').

LALME and eLALME 1.112 ('Language of Norfolk or Soke': ff. 36-57v).

LALME and eLALME LP 702; Grid 371 273 (NW Worcs; see LALME note 1.112).
LALME and eLALME 1.112 ('Language MHE, of an early type. Ireland').
LALME and eLALME LP 136; Grid 397 372 (Cheshire: Hand A); 1.112 (Cheshire: Hand B); LP 246; Grid 377 355 (Cheshire: Hand C); 1.112 (Cheshire: Hand D).
LALME and eLALME LP 6030; Grid 578 190 (Essex: ff. 1-67).
LALME and eLALME 1.113 (NME: Hand A, ff. 2-6v); 1.113 (NME: Hand B, ff. 7-132v); 1.113 (NMENME Hand C, ff. 133-164v); 1.113 (NME: Hand D, ff. 165-205v); 1.113 (NME: Hand E, ff. 206-258v).
LALME and eLALME LP 7600; Grid 391 277 (Worcs: Hand A, ff. 3-19, 52-81, 125 to end); LP 4681; Grid 410 262 (Warwicks: Hand B, ff. 40v-51v).
LALME and eLALME 1.113 ('Language possibly of SE Cambs').
LALME and eLALME 1.113 ('part III.…Language varies, but probably of Notts').
LALME and eLALME 1.113 ('in at least two hands. ff. 84r-91v appear to be in the language of NW Lincs').
argue for a London scribe copying a Western exemplar.
finds evidence for 'West Essex' or 'West Kent' (p. 151).
LALME and eLALME 1.114 ('Language probably of Norfolk' (ff. 1-66)).
LALME and eLALME LP 238; Grid 404 318 (Staffs: ff. 2-127v).
LALME and eLALME LP 509; Grid 461 330 (Notts: ff. 122v-143v).
LALME and eLALME 1.114 (Lincs: ff. 5v-10); LP 226; Grid 537 356 (Lincs: ff. 14-28v).
LAEME Index # 269; Grid 562 321 (Kings Lynn, NW Norfolk: Entry 1, Hand A, ff. 193–194v); Index # 270; Grid 562 321 (Kings Lynn, NW Norfolk: Entry 2, Hand B, f. 194v).
LALME and eLALME 1.115 ('…several hands, some probably from Durham County').

LALME and eLALME 1.115 (Staffs: f. 86v); LAEME Index # 260; Grid 367 276 (SE Salop: Entry 1, Hand A, ff. 1-8v: Sawles Warde (part); ff. 11-37, St Katherine; ff. 37-45v, St Margaret [part]); Index # 261; Grid 367 276 (SE Salop: Entry 2, Hand B, ff. 9-10v, Sawles Warde [end]; ff. 58v-70, St Juliana [end]; ff. 70r-v, Oreisun of Seinte Marie [incomplete]); Index # 262; Grid 367 276 (SE Salop: Entry 3, Hand C, ff. 45v beg. of paragraph 2 to 56, St Margaret [end]; ff. 56-58, St Juliana [beginning]).
LALME and eLALME LP 3; Grid 444 381 (Derbys: ff. 2, 13v-18v); LP 114; Grid 428 369 (Derbys: ff. 13v-18v).
LALME and eLALME LP 7260; Grid 350 218 (Herefords: ff. 4-115v); 1.115 (Derbys: ff. 150-183v).
LALME and eLALME 1.115 ('Cf. BL Royal 10 B. ix').
LALME and eLALME 1.115 ('Language looks to be of S Norfolk or N Suffolk').

believes the first scribe (ff. 1-166v) writes West Midlands English (pp. 158-9); the second scribe—the 'Hammond Scribe', ff. 167-301—evinces some 'Kentish features' and was probably 'an immigrant to London' (p. 158).
LALME and eLALME (part of) LP 6751; Grid 483 177 (Berks).
LALME and eLALME 1.115 ('Language mixed: elements of mid-Leics and ?SW Lincs').
'…the presence of certain diagnostic forms allow [sic] for a definite localisation within Norfolk' (, p. 161).
LALME and eLALME 1.116 ('Language of W Norfolk or possibly Ely; may be mixed').
LALME and eLALME LP 1.116 (NME: Hand A, ff. 1-8v); 1.116 ('Language northerly, possibly of Lincs': Hand B, f. 9); 1.116 (Notts: Hand C, f. 10 seq.).
LALME and eLALME LP 186; Grid 398 374 (Cheshire: ff. 150-183).
LALME and eLALME LP 9440; Grid 440 170 (Berks: ff. 43-44v).

LALME and eLALME LP 8320; Grid 592 254 (Suffolk: Hand A, ff. 4-21); 1.116 (Midland: Hand C, ff. 25-86v); 1.116 (Derbys or Cheshire: Hand E, ff. 91-96v); 1.116 (Staffs: Hand F, ff. 97-98v); 1.116 (Warwicks/Worcs: Hands H and I, ff. 102-141v); 1.116 ('…extreme NW Derbys or over into Cheshire': Hand J, ff. 142-146, 146v-147v); 1.116 (Midland: Hand K, f. 147v); 1.116 (Midland: Hand L, ff. 148-150v); 1.116 (Staffs: Hand M, f. 151r-v; LP 314; Grid 410 388 (Derbys: also Hand M, ff. 166-170); LP 718; Grid 403 368 (Staffs: Hand O, ff. 153-160); LP 729; Grid 398 349 (Staffs: Hand P, ff. 160-165v).

msDesc.lang: LAEME Index # 13 ('Localisation: text language not placed. There is very little diagnostic to go on. There is nothing to suggest northern language. DOP for DEEP and STUDES for STEAD pl. might point to the West Midlands.').

custodialHist.custEvent: Deposited at the National Library of Scotland in 1991; returned to the Scottish Catholic Archives in 2005

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME 1.88 (Norfolk); though, as notes, 'there are few traces of Norfolk usage in the tranches selected for analysis' (p. 150).
LALME and eLALME LP 411; Grid 398 399 (Lancs); LP 23; Grid 388 399 (Lancs).
LAEME Index # 259 ('text language not placed — too little to go on').

suggests 'the Essex/Suffolk border, although Norfolk is also a possible candidate' (p. 151). The MS may have originally belonged to Richard Doddington, priest of Foxearth, Essex (on the Essex/Essex border; see , 1.287-8; 1.618).

msIdentifier.idno: Covers of Old Law Books, MS 755

msDesc.head: Great Domesday Book of Ipswich (Percyvale's Domesday)

history.provenance: Sotheby's Sale, 1920; Hope Allen Addit. MS. III
Maggs Bros. Sale Cat. No. 580, Item 450

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME 1.98 ('may have a Northants element').

discerns in Scribe 1 (ff. 2-196) forms characteristic of 'Type III and Type IV forms,' that is language characteristic of London (p. 153). The second scribe (f. 196v to the end), also responsible for most of . In Lichfield 29, the scribe's spellings are mixed, suggesting provincial origins, though these marked forms may be exemplar-conditioned.
LALME and eLALME 1.98 (probably Cheshire: ff. 51-53, 54-57); (Derbys: ff. 89-121v).
LALME and eLALME 1.117 ('Language of Crowland area. Lincs').

adminInfo.note: Not LXV as listed in NIMEV

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME 1.117 ('MS associated with Adlingfleet, nr. Goole. WRY).

msDesc.lang: concludes that the 'cumulative weight of the…spelling features suggests strongly that they originated in northwest Essex or southwest Suffolk' (1.87).

msIdentifier.idno: Ailred of Rievaux, Life of Edward and Confessor

msDesc.lang: As in , London Type III and Type IV spellings comprise one layer, 'alongside a layer of West Midlands forms' (, p. 157; see also ). Both manuscripts are almost certainly London productions, as the hand also appears in London, Worshipful Company of Skinners, deposit, Guildhall Library MS 31692, ff. 2-22, from 21 Richard II-22 Henry VI (1443)=Register of Skinners/Book of the Fraternity of the Assumption of Our Lady.
LALME and eLALME LP 5390; Grid 415 130 (Wilts); LAEME Index # 258; Grid 413 130 (Salisbury, Wilts).
LALME and eLALME 1.162 (Derbys: part 1, ff. 1-28); 1.163 (Derbys: part 3, ff. 43-51v).
LALME and eLALME LP 80; Grid 350 284 (Salop [see note LALME 1.164-5]).
LAEME Index # 173; Grid 384 254 (Worcester, Worcs: Entry 1, ff. 1-63); Index # 172; Grid 384 254 (Worcester, Worcs: Entry 2, ff. 63, lines 14–28; 63v-66v).
LALME and eLALME LP 116; Grid 426 469 (WRY: 'Language clearly not of York, but plausibly of Ripon' [1.167]).

LALME and eLALME 1.168 (Norfolk: Hand C, ff. 108v-117v, 122v-125, 145v to end); 1.168 (Derbys: Hand D, ff. 118v-121); 1.168 (Notts: Hand E, ff. 129-144v); 1.168 ('Language is of S Salop or NE Herefords').

availability.p: Missing; considered lost since Raine's edition

University of Texas, Humanities Research Center

altIdentifier.idno: Chicago, Newberry Library, Louis H. Silver 2

msDesc.lang: The spellings are similar to those of Aberystwyth, National Library of Wales, MS Peniarth 393 and with some additional evidence of the influence of Type IV London English (see )

msIdentifier.repository: University of Texas, Humanities Research Center

msDesc.lang: localizes both scribes to Warwickshire, noting that 'Scribe A also shows the influence of Type IV (Chancery Standard)' while Scribe B has a layer of spellings indicative of East Anglia.

history.provenance: Robinson Sale Cat. 74, 1944, No. 268 and Robinson Sale Cat. 76, 1947, No. 233
Sotheby Sale, May 20, 1947, Lot 371; olim Le Neve
Sotheby's Sale, 1920, Lot 515; Hope Allen Addit. MS. IV

msDesc.lang: finds the spellings indicate 'Worcestershire, very near Pershire,' while 'other features (e.g., the splling '-th' for '-ght') suggest an East Anglian layer.' Cf. , who suggests 'West Oxfordshire' (p. 156).

msIdentifier.repository: Redemptorist Archives of the Baltimore Province

altIdentifier.idno: Mount St Alphonsus Seminary, Esopus, New York

history.provenance: olim Brooke (sold Sotheby's 1921); Harmsworth (sold Sotheby's 1945); George Smith of Marlborough (sold Sotheby's Feb. 2, 1960, Lot 317); Bodmer

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME LP 484 (NME: Hand A, pp. 1-61); LP 471 (NME: Hand B, pp. 62-94); LP 521 (NME: Hand C, pp. 95-284); 1.166 (NME: Hand D, p. 134); 1.166 (NME: Hand E, p. 138).

msDesc.head: Dinus de Mugello, De regulis iuris (Lyons, 1562)

msDesc.lang: finds that this manuscript shares the Southwest Midlands features also found in (by the same scribe), which is localized to Shropshire.

history.provenance: Katherine Fenwick; Thomas FitzRoy Fenwick; Alan George Fenwick; William H. Robinson Ltd., London; their sale, London, Sotheby's, July 17, 1950, lot 27; purchased for Curt F. Bühler at Sotheby's (London, Feb. 28, 1955, lot 169) by E.P. Goldschmidt, London

msDesc.lang: : 'either the…scribe copied exactly the spellings of his exemplar or…his own spelling system was very similar to that of []. Given that the [Lichfield 29] scribe's system is largely an uncomplicated mixture of Type III and Type IV forms it seems likely that the two scribes were both working in London with similar linguistic habits' (p. 156).
LALME and eLALME 1.140 (ᰰLanguage strongly northern, possibly of Cumberland. NME').

msDesc.head: The Ryal Book (trans. and printed William Caxton) (STC 21429)

msDesc.lang: The language of the first scribe (ff. 1-43) is colorless. The spellings of the second scribe (f. 43v-end) show a number of spellings of OE y realized as u; 'hur' for HER is common. These forms suggest the West Midlands. The spelling 'scho' for SHE, however, is primarily a Northern form and is found in the Southwest only in Gloucestershire, Shropshire, and Worcestershire ( 4.7). observes the presence of unetymological initial h- in 'holde' OLD and 'habraham' ABRAHAM. attests to this feature in Gloucestershire, Wiltshire, and Worcestershire in the Southwest, and also in East Anglia (4.320).
The same scribe as and , all with spellings that are markedly East Anglian.

altIdentifier.idno: Gilbert Ireland Blackburne of Hale Hall, Liverpool
Gilbert, Bishop of Bath and Wells

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME LP 559; Grid 541 282 (Ely: Hand A, ff. 1-146v); LP 551; Grid 535 317 (Lincs: Hand B, ff. 147-195v).

msDesc.lang: The same scribe as and , all with spellings that are markedly East Anglian.

msDesc.lang: Very similar to (and possibly the same scribe). Although this manuscript has associations with Kent, the language points to East Anglia.

msDesc.lang: LALME and eLALME (part of) LP 6400; Grid 532 183 (London).

adminInfo.note: Scottish Record Office GD 12 is a collection rather than a ms, I can't tell where this item is in it

msIdentifier.repository: Corporation of London Records Office [formerly Guildhall]

history.provenance: Dawsons of Pall Mall, Catalogue 103, item 3, 1970
Sotheby's, 10 Dec. 1969, Lot 43; acquired by Takamiya in 1976

msDesc.lang: See , which argues that southern Kent and northern Essex (Thames estuary) together accomodate the forms typical of this and other manuscripts by the same scribe(s) ('Hooked-g').

LALME and eLALME LP 5970; Grid 552 167 (Kent). But cf. Mosser 2004, which argues that the same scribe copied Cambridge University Library MS Gg.I.34 (Part 3), of Mandeville's Travels, which includes as LP 619 (Ely). Mosser concludes that Norfolk or Suffolk are more probable locations.
LALME and eLALME 1.161 ('Language is of S Lincs').

history.provenance: Goldschmidt Cat. 71, 1943, item 1, olim Greg, olim Bradfer-Lawrence 8, olim Huth 153
Foyle's sale at Christie's, 11 July 2000, lot 76
olim Quaritch Sale, 1931, item 82; Cat. 609, 1943, Lot 500; then Witten Cat., 1983, no. 22
Edwards of London Sale Cat. 624, item 247

Jean Mielot, Les quattres choses derrenieres (Bruges: William Caxton, 1475?)
A parchment broadsheet affixed to stretched canvas
